name: Punta Rata beach (Brela)
description: Pine-backed pebble beach in Brela on the Makarska Riviera, regularly ranked among Europe's best beaches. User-saved from Google Maps. June catch: parking ~€11/day closest zone (reports up to €25 and 'aggressive attendants') and a midday crush — slot it as the Jun 18 morning swim (also clears out after ~15:30); sunbed ~€7.
cost: Beach free; parking ~€11/day (reports up to €25); sunbed ~€7
bestTime: Day 3 (Jun 18) morning or after 15:30 — quick swim stop en route Makarska → Dubrovnik airport pickup. Midday is the crush.
communityNotes: "One of the most beautiful beaches in Croatia — pine trees down to the water, the colour is incredible." (TripAdvisor, multiple). Voted one of Europe's best beaches by Lonely Planet. Best early morning (pre-09:00) before the parasol crowd lands. Parking: the €11/day lot is a 10-min walk; the €25 lot is 2 min — the walk is worth it. Mid-June is manageable before the Croatian school holiday rush. June swim temp ~20–22°C. Sunbeds optional (€7), but the free shingle at the water's edge is fine.

name: Sveti Jure (Biokovo summit, 1762m)
description: Biokovo's highest peak (1,762m) — second-highest in Croatia. Drivable via the Biokovo NP toll road (car slot booking mandatory, fills up in June). At the summit: 360° panorama over the Makarska Riviera, all major Dalmatian islands (Brač, Hvar, Vis, Korčula), and the Apennines on clear days. Skywalk glass platform is on the same road (separate entry ~€15pp).
communityNotes: "On a clear day you can see Italy — the view is unlike anything else." Road booking: biokovo.hrvi.hr (max 300 cars/day, go early). NP entry ~€10pp + road access. Bring a jacket: wind and cold at 1,762m even in June. The drive itself through the clouds is an experience. The summit road ends at the telecom tower — 5-min walk to the cairn. Combine with the Skywalk stop on the way up. Best: leave Makarska 08:00, summit by 10:00 before afternoon haze.
cost: ~€10pp NP entry + road toll; car slot booking mandatory at biokovo.hrvi.hr
bestTime: Day 3 (Jun 18) early — leave Makarska 07:00, summit before 09:00 (the Skywalk slot is booked at shop.pp-biokovo.hr, 20-car/hour cap). Clear morning essential for the 360° Adriatic + island view.

name: Šćirovac / Miletnjak (Biokovo, 1619m)
description: 1,619m summit on the northwestern Biokovo massif, also known as Miletnjak — a Croatian Hiking Tour checkpoint (KT 17). Reached from Gornja Brela/Bartulovići via Bukovac mountain lodge and Pozjata cave. Full summit, not a drive-up viewpoint. 360° panorama: full Makarska Riviera, Brač/Hvar/Korčula/Vis, Bosnia inland.
communityNotes: Serious mountain: 4–4.5h one way from Gornja Brela (Bartulovići trailhead), ~1,400m gain. Trail markings faint for ~half the route — GPS/GPX track strongly recommended, 3–4L water per person, early start (pre-06:00). "Steep sides on both sides," genuinely hard ridge walk. Reward: views that include coast, 6 islands, Bosnia, Italy on clear days. Sources: planinarenje.hr, PeakVisor, Via Dinarica, AllTrails, Komoot/Wikiloc trails.
timeNeeded: 4–4.5h one way from Bartulovići (Gornja Brela); full day round trip

name: Camping Krvavica
description: Terraced pine-and-olive campsite at Krvavica between Baška Voda and Makarska — 200m walk to a pebble beach with clear water. Small (30 pitches), shaded, quiet. Hot showers, wifi, washing machine, electricity hookups, BBQ area. Cash only; nearest ATM in Promajna.
communityNotes: 'Small campsite 100 meters from the sea. Beautiful camping pitches.' 'Super nice little campsite very close to a beautiful beach — clean, quiet, shaded.' (TripAdvisor). park4night 4.37/5 from 230+ reviews (July 2025): clean showers praised consistently. Hot note: cash only, and book ahead in July–Aug.
cost: ~€55–65 for 4 + tent + car (June; per-person pricing: ~€12 adult + ~€8 tent + ~€4.50 car; electricity +€3–5)
facilities: hot showers, toilets, electricity, wifi, washing machine, BBQ — no restaurant on site

name: Montokuc viewpoint (Mljet NP)
description: The highest point in Mljet National Park — a forested summit trail giving panoramic views over the salt lakes (Malo and Veliko jezero), the sea, and the island interior. The trail starts near the Soline inlet and takes ~1.5–2h round-trip. Worth doing during the Mljet day to earn the lake views from above.
communityNotes: "Views from Montokuc over the lakes are stunning — completely different perspective than being at water level." Trail is well-marked from the NP reception area at Pristanište. Some exposure near the top but nothing technical. Carry water — shade is patchy. NP entry included in the day ticket (~€40pp). Best combined with a morning lake swim and afternoon return to mainland. "The combination of lake swim + hilltop view makes Mljet a proper full day."
bestTime: Day 4 (Jun 19) mid-afternoon — after the Mljet Lakes morning swim. Start by 15:00 for golden hour. 30 min moderate hike. Return ferry from Sobra next morning (06:00 or 09:00).

name: Veliki Prštavac waterfall (Plitvice)
description: One of the big waterfalls on Plitvice's upper lakes. User-saved from Google Maps — but the route verdict (verdicts-digest §1a) is a unanimous NO on Plitvice: +4:21 driving, ~€40 pp, timed boardwalks, no swimming, June crowds. Kept as candidate so the map doesn't imply a commitment nobody made.
communityNotes: Part of Plitvice NP which we rejected. Not on this trip.
